title: Enforce queue readiness invariant
goal: Harden queue reconciliation and validation so now cannot contain non-ready tasks and invalid lane placement is explained deterministically.

acceptance.assertion: A failing test proves a non-ready task in now is rejected or reported as a blocker by queue validation.
queue-reconcile emits the exact rule and proposed deterministic movement for invalid lane placement.
The invariant does not depend on AI, prose summaries or model classification.

metadata.advance_reason: BUREAU-DETERMINISTIC-OPS-V1-T001 is verified by this registry state; the next safe Bureau implementation ball is the deterministic queue readiness invariant.

verified_acceptance.now-readiness-check: PR #286 added a Registry.load / bureau check invariant rejecting any registry/queue.json now task whose registry state is not ready; tests/test_registry_queue.py covers the failure path.
verified_acceptance.repair-plan-evidence: PR #286 extended queue_reconcile findings with deterministic rule and proposed_action fields for now-not-ready, terminal queued, unqueued priority and lane-drift findings.
verified_acceptance.no-ai-evidence: The implementation is deterministic Python validation/reporting and test fixtures; no AI, Ollama, Gemini or prose model classification is used as evidence.

validation: PR #286 CI validate succeeded for Python 3.10 and 3.12 before merge.
Local validation before PR creation: PYTHONPATH=src make validate -> 391 passed.
